message.abstract:
<jats:title>Summary</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>What is already known about this topic?</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>SARS-CoV2 testing is a key component of a multi-layered mitigation strategy to enable safe return to in-person school for the K-12 population. However, costs, logistics, and uncertainty about effectiveness are potential barriers to implementation.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>What is added by this report?</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Over three months, 259,726 individual swabs were tested across 50,636 pools from 582 schools. Pool positivity rate was 0.8%; 98.1% of pools tested negative and 0.3% inconclusive, and 0.8% of pools submitted could not be tested. In reflex testing, 92.5% of fully deconvoluted pools with N1 or N2 target Ct ≤30 yielded a positive individual using the BinaxNOW antigen rapid diagnostic test (Ag RDT) performed 1-3 days later. With sufficient staffing support and low pool positivity rates, pooled sample collection and reflex testing were feasible for schools.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>What are the implications for public health practice?</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Screening testing for K-12 students and staff is achievable at scale and at low cost with a scheme that incorporates in-school pooling, RT-PCR primary testing, and Ag RDT reflex/deconvolution testing. Staffing support is a key factor for program success.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Implementation of SARS-CoV2 Screening in K-12 Schools using In-School Pooled Molecular Testing and Deconvolution by Rapid Antigen Test

reference.unstructured: News Division. Biden Administration to Invest More Than $12 Billion to Expand COVID-19 Testing. 2021. [Internet] URL: https://www.hhs.gov/about/news/2021/03/17/biden-administration-invest-more-than-12-billion-expand-covid-19-testing.html (Accessed 13 April 2021).

reference.article-title: A pooled testing strategy for identifying SARS-CoV-2 at low prevalence

reference.unstructured: Sinnott-Armstrong N , Klein DL , Hickey B. Evaluation of group testing for SARS-CoV-2 RNA.bioRxiv 2020. https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.03.27.20043968.

reference.article-title: Considerations for Group Testing: A Practical Approach for the Clinical Laboratory
